meta.title: later

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: later
root.mentor_note: later counts sides the way bi and tri count anything else: one, two, or many.

attempt.direction_prompt: Agreed by both sides, or decided by just one?

context_sentences: The company’s unilateral price increase caught even its own sales team by surprise.
Allies criticised the unilateral decision, made without a single round of consultation.
The two governments signed a bilateral trade agreement after eighteen months of negotiation.
A bilateral ceasefire held for exactly nine days before the first shots were fired again.
Twelve nations eventually joined the multilateral agreement on shared river access.
A multilateral summit, rather than a single bilateral meeting, was needed to settle the dispute.

construct_options.text: Involving three or more parties or sides acting together
Involving exactly two sides negotiating directly
Made by a single side, without any agreement from others

context_sentences: The physiotherapist tested his lateral movement first, then his balance moving forward and back.
A short lateral pass unlocked the defence in a way no forward run had managed all match.

construct_options.text: Relating to, or moving toward, the side of something
Relating to an agreement made by all parties at once
Moving directly forward without any change of direction
